question: A Megatron-LM functional recipe entry is flaky in the standard dev CI path, but I want to keep it discoverable and easy to re-enable. Answer in at most 5 bullets: the entry currently has `scope: [mr-github]`; what exact YAML change should I make, and how is this different from marking a unit test flaky in dev or LTS? You may read the relevant skill, but do not edit files.
ground_truth: The response should use the testing skill and say not to delete the recipe entry. For a functional recipe entry, disable it by suffixing the scope with -broken, so `scope: [mr-github]` becomes `scope: [mr-github-broken]`. It should explain that this preserves the entry for later re-enable and keeps the test discoverable. It should distinguish this recipe-scope mechanism from unit-test markers: use `@pytest.mark.flaky_in_dev` to skip a unit test in the dev environment and `@pytest.mark.flaky` to skip a unit test in the LTS environment. It should avoid suggesting unrelated workflow changes, deleting test cases, or editing golden values.
expected_behavior: Uses the testing skill guidance before answering.
Changes `scope: [mr-github]` to `scope: [mr-github-broken]` for the functional recipe entry.
Explains that the recipe entry should stay in place for discoverability and easy re-enable.
Distinguishes recipe `-broken` scopes from unit-test markers `@pytest.mark.flaky_in_dev` and `@pytest.mark.flaky`.
Does not recommend deleting the test case, removing the recipe entry, or changing golden values.
